name: Rosemary Chicken Wings
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 425°F. In a small saucepan, heat olive oil and butter over medium heat. Add shallots and rosemary and cook for 2 to 3 minutes. Add lemonade, black pepper, and salt. Simmer over low heat for 6 to 8 minutes or until slightly reduced and syrupy. Cool slightly.
Meanwhile, cut the chicken wings into three pieces, discarding the wing-tip joint. Place the wings in a shallow pan and coat well with the sauce. Bake in the oven until the skin is golden brown, about 30 minutes. Serve with rice or as hors d'oeuvre. Makes 20 to 24 pieces.
